title: Roth IRA vs Traditional IRA: Complete Comparison Guide
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Roth IRA vs Traditional IRA: Complete Comparison Guide. We compare Roth IRA vs Traditional IRA across key factors including costs, tax implications, and suitability to help you make the right choice.
excerpt: Should you choose Roth IRA or Traditional IRA? We break down the key differences, pros and cons, and help you determine which option best fits your financial goals.

content.introduction: Choosing between Roth IRA and Traditional IRA is one of the most common investment decisions. Both have distinct advantages and trade-offs, and the right choice depends on your personal financial situation, goals, and preferences. This comprehensive comparison examines every critical factor to help you make an informed decision.

leftSide.name: Roth IRA
points: No required minimum distributions (RMDs) in retirement
Contributions (not earnings) can be withdrawn anytime penalty-free
Income limits apply ($153K single, $228K married in 2024)
Ideal if you expect higher tax rates in retirement

rightSide.name: Traditional IRA
points: Contributions may be tax-deductible (lower taxes now)
Withdrawals taxed as ordinary income in retirement
Required minimum distributions start at age 73
No income limits for contributions (deduction may phase out)
Ideal if you expect lower tax rates in retirement

content.verdict: Choose Roth IRA if you are early in your career with lower current income and expect higher future tax rates. Choose Traditional IRA if you are in your peak earning years and want immediate tax savings. Many investors benefit from having both types for tax diversification in retirement.
